chapter_title: Toys, games and sports requisites; parts and accessories thereof
inclusions: Heading 95.03 — Tricycles, scooters, pedal cars and similar wheeled toys; dolls' carriages; dolls; other toys; reduced-size ('scale') models and similar recreational models, working or not; puzzles of all kinds. Note 4: heading 95.03 applies to toys combined with items that, if separate, would go elsewhere, provided put up for retail sale and the combination has essential character of toys. Note 5: does NOT cover articles identifiable as exclusively for animals (pet toys)

Heading 95.04 — Video game consoles and machines; articles for funfair, table or parlour games including pintables, billiards, special tables for casino games. Subheading 9504.50: (a) video game consoles reproducing image on external TV/monitor/screen; (b) video game machines with self-contained screen — EXCLUDES coin/banknote/token-operated machines (→ 9504.30)
Heading 95.05 — Festive, carnival or other entertainment articles, including conjuring tricks and novelty jokes

Heading 95.06 — Articles and equipment for general physical exercise, gymnastics, athletics, other sports (including table-tennis) or outdoor games; swimming pools and paddling pools. Includes: bows and arrows; archery equipment; fishing rods and tackle; ice/roller skates with boots attached; bobsleighs and toboggans; fencing equipment

Heading 95.07 — Fishing rods, fish-hooks and other line fishing tackle; fish landing nets, butterfly nets and similar nets; decoy 'birds' (other than of heading 92.08 or 97.05) and similar hunting or shooting requisites

Heading 95.08 — Roundabouts, swings, shooting galleries and other amusement park rides; travelling circuses and travelling menageries; travelling theatres. Per Note 6: 'amusement park rides' = devices carrying persons over fixed/restricted course or defined area for amusement (NOT residential/playground equipment). 'Water park amusements' = water-area specific devices (specially designed for water parks). 'Fairground amusements' = games of chance/strength/skill with operator (NOT 95.04 equipment)

exclusions.condition: Candles
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 34.06 — Note 1(a)

exclusions.condition: Fireworks or other pyrotechnic articles
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 36.04 — Note 1(b)

exclusions.condition: Yarns, monofilament, cords or gut for fishing, cut to length but NOT made up into fishing lines
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 39 (plastics), heading 42.06 (gut articles) or Section XI (textiles) — Note 1(c). Only MADE-UP fishing lines (with eyes, hooks, floats, etc.) → Ch.95; raw cut line material → by material

exclusions.condition: Sports bags or other containers
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 42.02 (sports bags of leather/plastic/textile), 43.03 (furskin containers) or 43.04 (artificial fur containers) — Note 1(d)

exclusions.condition: Fancy dress of textiles; sports clothing and special articles of apparel of textiles (whether or not incorporating incidental protective pads at elbow, knee or groin — e.g., fencing clothing, goalkeeper jerseys)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 61 (knitted apparel) or Chapter 62 (woven apparel) — Note 1(e). Textile sportswear → Ch.61/62 regardless of protective padding. Only non-textile protective equipment stays in Ch.95

exclusions.condition: Textile flags or bunting; sails for boats, sailboards or land craft
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 63 (made-up textile articles) — Note 1(f)

exclusions.condition: Sports footwear OTHER THAN skating boots with ice or roller skates ATTACHED; sports headgear
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 64 (footwear) or Chapter 65 (headgear) — Note 1(g). Exception: skating BOOTS with blades/wheels already attached → 95.06. Plain sports shoes without skates → Ch.64

exclusions.condition: Walking-sticks, whips, riding-crops or the like
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 66.02 (walking-sticks, whips, etc.) or 66.03 (parts) — Note 1(h)

exclusions.condition: Unmounted glass eyes for dolls or other toys
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 70.18 — Note 1(ij). Once mounted into the doll → part of toy (Ch.95)

exclusions.condition: Parts of general use (Note 2 to Section XV) of base metal, or similar goods of plastics
exclusions.action: Redirect to Section XV or Chapter 39 — Note 1(k)

exclusions.condition: Bells, gongs or the like
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 83.06 — Note 1(l)

exclusions.condition: Pumps for liquids (84.13); filtering/purifying machinery (84.21); electric motors (85.01); electric transformers (85.04); recording media/solid-state storage/smart cards (85.23); radio remote control apparatus (85.26); cordless infrared remote control devices (85.43)
exclusions.action: Redirect to respective headings in Ch.84 or Ch.85 — Note 1(m). These components remain in their own headings even when used in toys or games

exclusions.condition: Sports vehicles OTHER THAN bobsleighs, toboggans and the like
exclusions.action: Redirect to Section XVII (vehicles) — Note 1(n). Cars, motorcycles used for sport → Section XVII. Bobsleighs/toboggans (non-motorised) → 95.06

exclusions.condition: Children's bicycles
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 87.12 — Note 1(o). Children's BICYCLES (two wheels, pedal-driven) → 87.12 always. Children's OTHER CYCLES (tricycles, pedal cars) → 95.03

exclusions.condition: Unmanned aircraft (drones with utilitarian function)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 88.06 — Note 1(p). Drones with payload capability or permanently integrated camera for utilitarian use → 88.06. Flying toys solely for amusement → 95.03

exclusions.condition: Sports craft such as canoes and skiffs; their means of propulsion (paddles, oars of wood)
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 89 (vessels) or Chapter 44 (wooden paddles/oars) — Note 1(q). Kayaks, canoes, rowing boats used for sport → Ch.89

exclusions.condition: Spectacles, goggles or the like for sports or outdoor games
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 90.04 (spectacles and goggles) — Note 1(r). Sports goggles, swimming goggles, ski goggles → 90.04

exclusions.condition: Decoy calls or whistles
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 92.08 — Note 1(s). Bird calls, duck calls, game whistles → 92.08

exclusions.condition: Arms or other articles of Chapter 93
exclusions.action: Redirect to Chapter 93 — Note 1(t). Functional firearms, ammunition, edged weapons → Ch.93 even if used for sport

exclusions.condition: Lighting strings of all kinds (e.g., Christmas/fairy lights)
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 94.05 (luminaires and lighting fittings) — Note 1(u). Decorative lighting strings → 94.05 not Ch.95

exclusions.condition: Monopods, bipods, tripods and similar articles
exclusions.action: Redirect to heading 96.20 — Note 1(v)

exclusions.condition: Racket strings; tents or other camping goods; gloves, mittens and mitts
exclusions.action: Classify according to constituent material — Note 1(w). Tennis/badminton racket strings → textile or plastics heading. Tents → Ch.63. Gloves/mitts → by material (leather → Ch.42, textile → Ch.61/62)

exclusions.condition: Tableware, kitchenware, toilet articles, carpets, apparel, bed/table/toilet/kitchen linen and similar articles having a UTILITARIAN FUNCTION
exclusions.action: Classify according to constituent material — Note 1(x). Sports-themed tableware, novelty kitchenware → functional articles classified by material, not as toys. Only purely decorative/play articles → Ch.95

classification_rules.rule: Note 2 — Precious metal/stones as minor constituents do NOT move articles out of Ch.95
classification_rules.description: Chapter 95 includes articles that contain natural/cultured pearls, precious/semi-precious stones, precious metal or metal clad with precious metal — provided these constitute only MINOR CONSTITUENTS of the article. A toy or game with small gemstone decoration → stays in Ch.95. Only if precious materials give the article its essential character → Ch.71.

classification_rules.rule: Note 3 — Parts and accessories: sole/principal use test for Ch.95
classification_rules.description: Subject to Note 1 exclusions, parts and accessories suitable for use solely or principally with articles of Ch.95 → classified with those articles within Ch.95. This gives Ch.95 broad pull-in power for dedicated accessories. But general-purpose parts (Note 2 to Section XV) and specific excluded goods (electric motors, remote controls, etc.) override this rule.

classification_rules.rule: Note 4 — Toys combined with other items: 95.03 applies if essential character is toy
classification_rules.description: Heading 95.03 applies to toys presented together with other items (which separately would go to other headings) IF: (a) put up together for retail sale; AND (b) the combination has the ESSENTIAL CHARACTER of a toy. These combinations are NOT GRI 3(b) sets. Example: a doll presented with a comb and mirror → 95.03 if toy character dominates. A chemistry set with real chemicals → depends on whether the toy element dominates.

Heading 95.03 does NOT cover articles that by design, shape or constituent material are identifiable as EXCLUSIVELY intended for animals (pet toys). Pet chew toys, bird toys, cat scratching toys → classified in their appropriate heading (usually by material). Human-use toys → 95.03.

classification_rules.rule: Note 6 and Subheading Note 1 — Heading 95.08 and 9504.50 scope definitions
classification_rules.description: 95.08 amusement park rides: carry persons over fixed/restricted course or defined area (includes water courses) for amusement — does NOT include residential or playground equipment. Water park amusements: water-area devices specifically designed for water parks. Fairground amusements: games of chance/skill/strength with operator. 9504.50 (video game consoles/machines): (a) consoles outputting to external screen; (b) machines with self-contained screen. Coin/token-operated → 9504.30.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 95.03 (children's toys) vs 87.12 (children's bicycles) vs 88.06 (drones)
classification_rules.description: Three specific carve-outs from Ch.95 for child/toy-like vehicles: children's BICYCLES (2 wheels, pedals) → always 87.12. All OTHER non-motorised children's cycles (tricycles, scooters, pedal cars) → 95.03. Unmanned aircraft with utilitarian function (camera drone) → 88.06. Flying toys solely for amusement → 95.03. The key tests: (1) Is it a bicycle? → 87.12. (2) Is it a UAV with utilitarian function? → 88.06. (3) Everything else → 95.03.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: 95.06 (sports equipment) vs Ch.61/62 (textile sportswear) vs Ch.64 (sports footwear)
classification_rules.description: Textile sports CLOTHING (jerseys, suits, shorts) → Ch.61/62, even with built-in protective pads at elbow/knee/groin. Sports FOOTWEAR → Ch.64, EXCEPT skating boots with ice blades or roller skates already attached → 95.06. Sports EQUIPMENT (not clothing/footwear) → 95.06. The attachment test for skating boots is binary: boots without skates → Ch.64; boots WITH skates fixed → 95.06.

classification_rules.rule: Key boundary: Ch.95 (toys/games/sports) vs Ch.93 (functional arms) for sporting weapons
classification_rules.description: Bows and arrows for archery/sport → 95.06. Functional firearms used in shooting sports (rifles, shotguns) → Ch.93. Fencing foils (sport) → 95.06. Bayonets and combat knives → Ch.93. The dividing line is functional lethality as a weapon: purpose-built sports equipment that cannot kill effectively → Ch.95; functional weapons used competitively → Ch.93.
